purpose: Enable an Admin to upload, update, validate, and maintain AEON branding assets including logo files, color palette definitions, and font packages so they can be consistently applied to customer-facing templates, generated documents, and exports.
trigger: Admin opens the Branding and Templates administration area and submits new or updated branding assets for tenant use.
preconditions: The actor is authenticated as an Admin within the tenant context.
The tenant exists and is active.
The Admin has permission to manage branding configuration.
At least one branding management entry point is available through the admin console screen scr-admin-branding-templates.
Storage for branding assets is available and writable.
Branding and template administration changes are audit logged.
Document generation templates exist or will consume branding settings after successful update.
inputSource: Admin UI screen scr-admin-branding-templates invoking api-branding-assets-upload via multipart form submission with tenant and user context.
output: Updated tenant branding asset configuration with persisted asset references, versioned metadata, validation outcome, and audit evidence available for downstream template and document generation processes.
mainFlow: Admin navigates to the Branding and Templates administration screen.
System loads the current tenant branding configuration and allows the Admin to select updated branding inputs such as logo file, colors JSON, and fonts ZIP.
Admin submits one or more branding inputs.
System validates that the request is associated with an authenticated Admin and a valid tenant context.
System checks that at least one branding input is present in the request.
System validates the uploaded files and structured color configuration for allowed formats, file size, and content integrity.
System stores new branding files in secure storage and prepares normalized metadata for persistence.
System creates or updates the tenant branding record in the branding asset store.
System increments version and updates etag-style concurrency metadata for the branding asset record.
System records an immutable audit entry describing the branding change, actor, timestamps, and before or after values as applicable.
System returns the updated branding asset status and metadata to the Admin.
Subsequent template rendering and document generation flows use the updated branding assets for customer-facing outputs.
alternativeFlow: Admin uploads only a logo file while retaining previously saved colors and fonts; system updates only the logo reference and preserves other existing branding elements.
Admin uploads only colorsJson; system updates palette configuration without changing stored logo or fonts.
Admin uploads only fontsZip; system updates font package reference while leaving logo and colors unchanged.
A tenant has no prior branding configuration; system creates the initial BrandingAsset record instead of updating an existing one.
Admin replays the same request with the same idempotency key; system returns the previously committed result without creating duplicate updates.
Branding assets are uploaded successfully but are not yet consumed by any generated document; system still persists the configuration for future document generation use.
exceptionFlow: If the actor is not authenticated or tenant context is missing, the system rejects the request and records the failed access attempt.
If the actor lacks Admin privileges or branding management permission, the system denies the action and audits the authorization failure.
If no branding input is provided, the system rejects the request as invalid because at least one of logoFile, colorsJson, or fontsZip must be supplied.
If the uploaded logo file or fonts archive fails file validation, antivirus, or content checks, the system rejects the update and preserves the prior branding configuration.
If colorsJson is malformed or does not conform to expected color configuration structure, the system rejects the request and returns validation details.
If storage upload fails or the database update cannot be committed, the system rolls back the branding update and returns an error.
If an unexpected system exception occurs during persistence or audit logging, the system records diagnostic logs, returns a failure response, and leaves the previous branding state unchanged.
validationRules: tenantId must reference an existing active tenant.
adminUserId must reference an authenticated active user.
The acting user must hold the Admin role or equivalent branding management permission.
At least one of logoFile, colorsJson, or fontsZip must be supplied.
logoFile, when present, must be an allowed image format supported by the branding workflow.
fontsZip, when present, must be a valid ZIP archive containing supported font files.
colorsJson, when present, must be valid JSON and parse successfully.
colorsJson should contain valid color values in approved format such as hex codes for configured brand color keys.
Uploaded files must pass content validation and malware scanning before being committed for use.
Request must include a non-empty idempotencyKey for safe retry behavior.
businessRules: Branding changes are tenant-scoped and must not affect other tenants.
Branding and template administration changes do not require dual approval but must be fully audit logged.
The latest successful branding configuration becomes the active source for customer-facing document rendering.
If only part of the branding payload is supplied, unspecified branding components retain their previously active values.
Failed branding updates must not partially overwrite the active branding configuration.
Branding assets are used across customer-facing templates and generated documents in supported export formats.
Only Admin users can manage branding assets.
Idempotent retries must not create duplicate branding versions for the same logical request.
externalServicesUsed: Object storage or NAS for branding file persistence
Antivirus or file validation service used by ingestion pipeline
configurationRequired: Allowed logo MIME types and file extensions
Allowed font file types within ZIP archives
Maximum upload size for branding assets
Tenant-scoped secure storage location for branding files
Audit retention policy for branding administration events
Template rendering configuration that resolves active BrandingAsset for a tenant
loggingRequired: Log request correlation id, tenant id, actor user id, validation outcomes, storage operations, database update result, and any failure diagnostics without logging sensitive file contents.
retryRequirement: Client retries are supported through idempotency keys. Storage and transient persistence failures may be retried once internally when safe, but duplicate logical branding updates must be prevented.
transactionRequirement: Database persistence of BrandingAsset metadata and audit creation should occur atomically. File storage and metadata update should follow a coordinated commit pattern so failed persistence does not leave an inconsistent active branding state.
